Entered the market in 2017, got liquidated three times and went from 20k to just 1500U. I even questioned myself in the middle of the night, countless times wanting to give up.
 
Later, I realized that the crypto market isn't about luck; all losses stem from having no bottom line and no rules.
 
I kicked the habit of going all-in, stopped trying to catch the bottom, and quit chasing pumps. I set three hard rules:
✅ Use the 60-day moving average to define trends; no guessing tops or catching bottoms
✅ Don't chase coins that have pumped over 50%; only profit within my understanding
✅ Better to stay in cash and miss out than to blindly enter and lose principal
 
From liquidation and sinking to stable profits was just about finding my own trading rhythm.
 
Don't self-doubt in losses; what you lack isn't luck, it's a survival rule set. First seek survival, then aim for profit; this is the simplest truth of the market. #BTC #ETH

translatedData.content:
⚡ Why $ETH  can't move anymore — The honest answer

Ethereum is stuck. Not for a week. Not for a month. But for years. Meanwhile, smaller, weaker coins keep multiplying tenfold. Ask any ETH holder how that feels. Let’s break down why.

🪙 Supply Issues
ETH doesn’t have a hard cap. It keeps minting new tokens for validators every day. EIP-1559 burns a portion, but during low activity phases, the burn doesn’t keep up. The net result — ETH is currently a bit inflationary. Bitcoin halves its issuance every four years. ETH does not. Math matters over time.

🐋 Whale Pressure
Early investors who bought at $1 or $50 still hold large amounts of tokens. Whenever ETH pumps significantly, they sell when the price is hot. Not out of malice — just sound strategy. But this creates a price ceiling that smaller coins don’t face.

🏗️ Identity Crisis
Is ETH money? Tech stock? Or a commodity? Bitcoin has a clear narrative — digital gold. $SOL has a clear narrative — fast and cheap. ETH has up to 100 narratives at once, making it hard for the market to focus on a single story.

💸 Layer 2 Tax
Ethereum scales by shifting activity to Layer 2. Arbitrum, Base, Optimism generate billions in volume. But the value accrued mainly belongs to those L2 tokens, not ETH. It becomes the payment layer everyone uses, but few are willing to pay much for.

📊 ETF Reality
Spot ETH ETFs have existed. There’s inflow. But it’s just a fraction compared to $BTC ETFs. Institutional capital still sees ETH as a secondary bet.
#ETH $ETH

content:
⚡ Tại sao $ETH không thể di chuyển nữa — Câu trả lời trung thực

Ethereum đang bị kẹt. Không phải một tuần. Không phải một tháng. Mà là nhiều năm. Trong khi đó, các đồng coin nhỏ hơn và yếu hơn vẫn liên tục tăng gấp 10 lần. Hãy hỏi bất kỳ người nắm giữ ETH nào cảm giác đó như thế nào. Hãy cùng nói về lý do tại sao.

🪙 Vấn đề về nguồn cung
ETH không có giới hạn cứng. Nó tiếp tục phát hành token mới cho các validator mỗi ngày. EIP-1559 đốt một phần, nhưng trong những giai đoạn hoạt động thấp, lượng đốt không theo kịp. Kết quả ròng — ETH hiện đang hơi lạm phát. Bitcoin giảm một nửa lượng phát hành mỗi bốn năm. ETH thì không. Toán học quan trọng theo thời gian.

🐋 Áp lực từ cá voi
Những nhà đầu tư sớm mua ở giá $1 hoặc $50 vẫn giữ lượng lớn token. Mỗi khi ETH tăng giá đáng kể, họ bán ra khi giá mạnh. Không phải ác ý — mà là hợp lý. Nhưng điều này tạo ra một trần giá mà các đồng coin nhỏ không có.

🏗️ Khủng hoảng định danh
ETH là tiền? Là cổ phiếu công nghệ? Hay là hàng hóa? Bitcoin có câu chuyện rõ ràng — vàng kỹ thuật số. $SOL có câu chuyện rõ ràng — nhanh và rẻ. ETH có đến 100 câu chuyện cùng lúc, nên thị trường không thể tập trung vào một câu chuyện duy nhất.

💸 Thuế Lớp 2
Ethereum mở rộng bằng cách chuyển hoạt động sang Layer 2. Arbitrum, Base, Optimism tạo ra hàng tỷ đô la khối lượng. Nhưng giá trị thu được chủ yếu thuộc về các token L2 đó, không phải ETH. Nó trở thành lớp thanh toán mà ai cũng dùng nhưng ít ai trả nhiều tiền.

📊 Thực tế ETF
Các quỹ ETF spot ETH đã tồn tại. Dòng tiền vào có. Nhưng chỉ là một phần nhỏ so với ETF $BTC. Vốn tổ chức vẫn coi ETH là cược phụ.
#ETH $ETH

translatedData.content:
After trading contracts for so many years, I've seen too many people not lose to the market, but rather fall victim to "not understanding the rules" #COS 
Just opened a position only to go against it?
Stop-loss triggered and then the price shoots up?
You had the right direction, but your account still hits zero?
Stop doubting whether you're being "targeted". #q 
The truth is quite harsh:
Contracts are essentially a massive betting game #ETH 
You think you're trading, but you're actually playing against the exchange and everyone else in the market.
The money you make has to come from someone else's losses;
The money you lose will never just vanish into thin air.
Many newbies easily overlook funding rates.
They see that tiny number and think it doesn't matter.
But those who truly understand know:
Consistently high funding rates often mean extreme market sentiment.
Once retail traders rush in, a market reversal can pull the rug out from under you.
And then there's leverage.
Many only see their profits doubling, but fail to see that the risk is also amplified simultaneously.
Fees, funding rates, spreads, liquidation prices…
Each layer feels like a blade.
You think you're making money, but your account is slowly bleeding out.
The hardest hit is liquidation.
Many believe that with 10x leverage, they can withstand a 10% fluctuation.
Wrong.
The real liquidation mechanism won't even allow you to reach that position.
With just a slight market shake, your position could already be gone.
There's another way to get wrecked called "rolling over positions".
You make a profit on one trade, then go all in on the next.
Thinking you've entered a wealth accelerator.
But when the market flips, your profits and capital get swallowed whole.
So I set a strict rule for myself:
You must take half of the profits, and only then can you keep gambling with the rest.
Because surviving is more important than anything else.
Stop always dreaming of overnight riches.
The strongest players in this market are never those who make the quickest gains, but those who endure the longest.
I'm not some trading god.
I just got into the game a bit earlier, blown up too many positions, and slowly learned the rules.
Brothers, if you really want to survive in contracts, first learn to respect the market.
Don't keep risking real money on tuition fees that could have been avoided.
Trying to go it alone makes it easy to get lost.

错。

利润必须拿走一半，剩下的才能继续博。

If your account skyrockets from 100k U to 9 million in just a few days, can you control the greed to take profits in U? $Q

In 2024's market wave, I went all in on TRB with 100k USD. In just a few months, my account soared to over 3 million, and I was on cloud nine.
The first thing I did every morning was check my balance; I even had my Porsche color picked out.
Then #TRB  came the final crazy surge.
During those days around New Year's, the price shot up to 555 U, and my account suddenly hit 8 digits.
9 million was right in front of me.
But I didn't sell #ARB 
Because I always felt it could go higher.
Then, in the blink of an eye, the market taught me a lesson. #Saga 
A massive bearish candlestick came crashing down, profits wildly retraced, and my position spiraled out of control. In just a few days, most of what I had earned was taken back by the market.
That night, staring at the screen, I fully understood one thing:
Those who can buy are the apprentices; those who can sell are the masters.
Since then, I set three hard rules for myself #ETH 
First: Take profits in stages.
When it hits your target, pull back your principal, then slowly lock in the profits. Leave the remaining position to the trend, instead of fantasizing about selling at the peak.
Second: Set stop-losses in advance.
In a real bull market, there won't be time for hesitation. Once the market goes out of control, execution is always more important than judgment.
Third: Never eat the last bite.
I don’t want the fish head, and I won’t fight for the tail; I only eat the plump, stable part in the middle.
Over the years, I've seen too many people.
They made dozens of times their investment, but because they couldn't let go, they ended up giving it all back to the market.
It’s not that they can’t make money; it’s that they can’t stop.
Now, my biggest goal in trading isn't to get rich quick.
It's to survive long-term.
Because only the money that’s in your pocket truly belongs to you.
The hardest part in crypto isn’t finding the right entry.
It’s staying clear-headed when everyone else is going wild.
If you've ever stumbled into the pit of 'earning but not holding,' you should understand this sentiment.

Volume and price action are key: if you see a breakout on low volume, get in; if high volume isn’t pushing prices up, clear out to avoid getting 'reaped'. Only trade coins in an upward trend: use the 3-day MA for short-term opportunities, the 30-day MA for mid-term plays, and when the 80-day MA signals, go heavy for that bullish wave; the 120-day MA will show you the overall bull market direction. 2) Order-size rule (avoid rejections & “dust”): Every pair has trading parameters (min order value, quantity step size, price tick size). I always adjust my ETH amount to the allowed decimals so the order goes through cleanly—and I don’t end up with small leftover ETH I can’t easily sell.
3) Exit rule (risk first): Before I buy, I already know where I’m wrong (invalidation/stop level) and I keep risk small (example: 1–2% max). If the setup breaks, I exit—no hope trading.
 
This is spot-only discipline (no leverage). Not financial advice—just my process.
